payload.subject: Work order: build bootstrap_state_db.py — SQLite state layer bootstrap script (turn 42)
payload.summary: New work stream: SQLite state layer for the psychology agent. Schema committed at scripts/schema.sql. Requesting psq-sub-agent build the bootstrap script that seeds the DB from existing markdown and transport JSON files. This parallels the schema/integration work psychology-agent will do on skills and hooks.

context.purpose: Queryable structured state alongside markdown files. Reduces per-session token usage by ~57% (estimated) by replacing full file reads with targeted SQL queries. Positions the project for autonomous operation.
context.architecture: Phase 1: markdown = source of truth, DB = queryable index. Phase 2 (future): DB = source of truth, markdown = derived view.
context.db_location: state.db in project root (gitignored — rebuilt from bootstrap script)

spec.language: Python 3.10+ (stdlib only — sqlite3, json, pathlib, re, datetime)
spec.no_dependencies: Zero external packages. Must run on any machine with Python 3.10+.
behavior: Read scripts/schema.sql and execute it to create all tables (idempotent — uses IF NOT EXISTS)

Parse transport/sessions/**/from-*.json → INSERT into transport_messages (extract metadata fields from JSON top-level: session_id, turn, message_type, from.agent_id, to.agent_id, timestamp, payload.subject, claims count, setl, urgency)
Parse docs/architecture.md Design Decisions table → INSERT into decision_chain (extract decision name, choice text, decided date from the APA-style text table between the ──── delimiters)

Parse memory topic files (decisions.md, cogarch.md, psq-status.md from docs/memory-snapshots/) → INSERT into memory_entries (topic from filename, entry_key from bold-prefixed lines like **Key:** value, status from ✓/✗/⚑ markers)
Parse lab-notebook.md session headers (## YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM TZ — Session N (summary)) → INSERT into session_log
Parse docs/cognitive-triggers.md trigger definitions → INSERT into trigger_state (trigger_id from T{N} pattern, description from heading text)
Set all transport_messages.processed = TRUE (historical messages already reviewed)
Set all memory_entries.last_confirmed to today's date (baseline — decay tracking starts from this point)
Print summary: row counts per table, any parse warnings
error_handling: Continue on individual parse failures — log warning, skip entry, report at end
Fail hard only if schema.sql cannot be read or DB cannot be created
If state.db already exists, prompt user (--force flag to overwrite without prompting)
testing: Run on the current repo state and verify: ~50 transport messages indexed, ~20 design decisions, ~80 memory entries, 47 session log entries, 16 triggers
Verify round-trip: query transport_messages WHERE from_agent='psq-sub-agent' should return ~21 rows
Verify decision_chain contains 'PSQ structural model' entry with derives_from linking
notes: The architecture.md decisions table uses a non-standard APA text format (fixed-width columns between ──── delimiters). The parser needs to handle multi-line decision entries where continuation lines are indented.

Transport JSON files have consistent top-level schema (interagent/v1). The payload structure varies per message_type but the metadata fields (turn, message_type, from, to, timestamp) are always present.

Memory topic files use heterogeneous formats — psq-status.md uses **bold key:** value lines, decisions.md uses a markdown table, cogarch.md uses mixed headers and lists. The parser should handle each topic file format specifically rather than using a single generic parser.
The script should be runnable from the project root: python scripts/bootstrap_state_db.py

claims.text: scripts/schema.sql has been committed to the repo and defines 7 tables: transport_messages, memory_entries, decision_chain, trigger_state, session_log, claims, epistemic_flags, plus schema_version.

action_gate.gate_note: Psychology-agent will begin /sync integration (SL-2) after the bootstrap script lands and passes validation.

epistemic_flags: The architecture.md parser will face the most complexity — the text table format uses fixed-width columns with multi-line entries. A regex-based approach may miss edge cases. Acceptable for bootstrap (can be re-run after fixes).

Memory topic file parsing depends on current file formats. If formats change before the script runs, the parser needs updating. The committed snapshot files (docs/memory-snapshots/) provide a stable input source.

Token savings estimate (57%) has not been validated empirically. Actual savings depend on how effectively the agent uses SQL queries instead of file reads — a behavioral change that requires skill/hook integration (SL-2+).
